authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 164.508 - Uses and disclosures for which an authorization is required
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal regulatory text for HIPAA authorization requirements. It directly supports disclosure-readiness checks, missing authorization handling, and unsupported release refusal.

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR Part 164 Subpart D - Notification in the Case of Breach of Unsecured Protected Health Information
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal regulatory text for breach-notification duties. It anchors breach-sensitive incident triage while requiring escalation for final determinations.

authoritative_sources.title: Summary of the HIPAA Privacy Rule
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA privacy regulator. The summary grounds permitted uses/disclosures, individual rights, safeguards, administrative requirements, and privacy officer boundaries.

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR guidance directly governs limiting PHI uses, disclosures, and requests to the reasonably necessary scope, supporting minimum-necessary refusal scenarios.

authoritative_sources.title: Individuals' Right under HIPAA to Access their Health Information
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR guidance defines right-of-access expectations, designated record set handling, and patient-access workflow boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: access request readiness and patient access routing

authoritative_sources.title: Right to an Accounting of Disclosures
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR guidance grounds accounting-of-disclosures evidence, exceptions, disclosure log requirements, and HIM/privacy routing.

authoritative_sources.title: 45 CFR 164.514 - Other requirements relating to uses and disclosures of protected health information
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal regulatory text for verification of identity and authority, minimum-necessary implementation, limited data sets, and related disclosure controls.
used_for: limited data set and scope escalation

authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Basics for Providers: Privacy, Security, and Breach Notification Rules
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS provider education translates HIPAA privacy, security, and breach requirements into covered-provider operating expectations and common workflow terms.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: provider privacy operations and staff-facing evidence handling

authoritative_sources.title: Information Blocking
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / Office of the National Coordinator for Health IT
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASTP/ONC administers information-blocking policy for health IT actors. It is authoritative for EHI access, exchange, and use boundaries that may intersect patient access and privacy operations.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-66 Revision 2: Implementing the HIPAA Security Rule
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST is a federal standards body. SP 800-66 Rev. 2 is a recognized HIPAA Security Rule implementation guide for ePHI safeguards, audit, access, and evidence expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: ePHI audit, access, and security-control evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: HL7 FHIR AuditEvent Resource
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HL7 FHIR is a recognized healthcare interoperability standard. AuditEvent grounds vendor-neutral audit-event terminology for access-event triage and traceability.

authoritative_sources.title: Release of Information Toolkit
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHIMA is a recognized health information management professional body. Its ROI toolkit grounds release-of-information workflow terminology and HIM adjacency for disclosure handling.

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Required prerequisites include confirmed patient/member identity, requester identity and authority, recipient, purpose, requested PHI scope, date range, disclosure basis, authorization status, consent or representative record, restriction/amendment status, minimum-necessary constraint, tenant privacy policy, jurisdiction, facility or business unit, service setting, source-of-truth hierarchy, and responsible privacy/HIM/legal/security owner.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: Truth-set systems include privacy incident management, EHR encounter and audit logging, release-of-information systems, patient portal/access request queues, identity and access management, case management, disclosure logs, authorization repositories, document repositories, policy and training systems, legal/security ticketing, payer portals, and eligibility/authorization platforms. Tenant source hierarchy decides which record wins when they conflict.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before legal advice, final HIPAA/state-law interpretation, final breach determination, regulator notification decisions, unsupported disclosure approval, waiver of controls, clinical record amendment judgment, diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical safety triage, coverage determination, payer policy interpretation, medical-necessity interpretation, coding, claims disposition, or authorization approval.

workflow_stages.name: Privacy intake and boundary classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture request intent, patient/member context, requester, recipient, purpose, PHI scope, disclosure/access/incident type, tenant policy context, jurisdiction, and authority boundary before treating the request as actionable.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and authorization validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate identity proofing, representative authority, authorization elements, consent or restriction posture, purpose, scope, date range, recipient, service dates, disclosure basis, and minimum-necessary constraints.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Systems-of-record and evidence assembly
workflow_stages.description: Collect disclosure logs, ROI records, EHR audit events, IAM context, incident notes, authorization forms, patient access queues, amendment/restriction requests, training attestations, policy references, and legal/security ticket records.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Control mapping and review preparation
workflow_stages.description: Map the request or incident to HIPAA/privacy controls, tenant policy, minimum-necessary handling, identity/access requirements, disclosure/accounting requirements, evidence owners, review cadence, and missing information.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception, remediation, and escalation routing
workflow_stages.description: Escalate incomplete or conflicting authorization, PHI exposure, suspected breach, security/IAM evidence gaps, legal interpretation, patient access/HIM response, clinical amendment judgment, payer dependency, or tenant/public policy conflict.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Traceable output and closure readiness
workflow_stages.description: Produce privacy-review intake summaries, incident triage notes, evidence checklists, control summaries, and exception escalation notes while avoiding final signoff unless the responsible owner has documented authority and closure evidence.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

artifacts: Amendment requests and HIM review records
EHR audit logs and access events
Patient portal and access request records
Legal, HIM, security, patient access, payer, and compliance routing notes
systems_of_record: EHR audit logging and encounter systems
Patient portal and patient access request queue
Case management or compliance ticketing system
Authorization, consent, restriction, and personal representative repository
Document management and designated record set repository
Learning management and policy attestation systems
Payer portals and eligibility or authorization platforms

allowed: Classify healthcare privacy requests as in scope, blocked, refused, or adjacent-owner work.
Determine whether access, disclosure, accounting, amendment, restriction, or incident requests are complete enough for privacy review.
Map obligations and controls to evidence needs, review cadence, missing tenant facts, and responsible owners.
Classify incidents for privacy investigation intake, workforce follow-up, security/IAM evidence gathering, legal escalation, HIM/ROI routing, or compliance tracking.
Prepare privacy-review intake summaries, incident triage notes, control summaries, evidence packets, and exception escalation notes with traceability.
escalate: Tenant jurisdiction, facility policy, source hierarchy, role map, authorization form standard, identity proofing rule, or retrieval hook is missing.
EHR audit, ROI, IAM, privacy incident, patient portal, disclosure log, or case management systems materially conflict.
PHI handling, consent, representative authority, restriction status, Part 2 data, state privacy law, or minimum-necessary interpretation changes the answer.
Suspected breach requires legal/privacy officer determination, notification decision, sanctions decision, or regulator-facing communication.
Clinical amendment, patient-safety, diagnosis, treatment, medication, or clinical risk question requires licensed clinical or HIM authority.
Payer eligibility, coverage, authorization, referral, medical-necessity, coding, claim, or payer-policy interpretation changes the response.
Public baseline and tenant policy conflict materially.
refuse: Request asks for legal advice, final breach determination, final HIPAA/state-law interpretation, formal certification, or regulator-facing signoff.
Request asks to approve unsupported disclosure, release PHI without verified authority, or bypass identity verification.
Request asks to waive minimum-necessary, role-based access, consent, restriction, or audit controls for convenience.
Request asks for diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical amendment judgment, licensed clinical advice, payer coverage decision, medical-necessity interpretation, or authorization approval.
Request asks to close an incident, accounting request, access event, or exception when evidence, authority, mitigation, or owner decision is incomplete.

domain_failure_modes: PHI exposure from disclosing records without valid authorization, verified identity, representative authority, or tenant-approved disclosure basis.
Privacy violation from treating operational convenience as a minimum-necessary exception or exporting more PHI than the stated purpose supports.
Patient or member harm from bad routing, missed privacy restriction, unresolved amendment request, or unrecognized clinical safety signal embedded in a privacy request.
Incomplete breach trail because EHR audit events, IAM records, mitigation notes, role context, or patient impact evidence were not collected before escalation.
False confidence from making final breach, legal, or regulator notification determinations outside the lane authority.
Payer or revenue exposure from mishandling payer record requests without service dates, eligibility, authorization, referral, or coverage context.
Incorrect accounting-of-disclosures response because disclosure logs, ROI records, and audit events conflict and tenant source hierarchy is missing.
Unlawful or unsupported release because Part 2, state law, reproductive health, minor consent, behavioral health, or tenant policy constraints were not escalated when conflicting.
Lost auditability because incident triage, disclosure review, evidence needs, owner routing, and closure state were not recorded in the designated system of record.
Improvised cross-functional ownership when legal, HIM/ROI, security, patient access, licensed clinical, payer operations, or compliance should own the decision.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Disclosure request lacks valid authorization or identity proofing
Access event may be a breach but evidence is incomplete
Operational convenience conflicts with minimum-necessary handling
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation
Tenant-specific adaptation preserving public baseline and recording assumptions
Request for formal legal interpretation or signoff outside lane authority
Safe escalation when patient safety, clinical judgment, or licensed review is required
Exception and remediation handling for workforce or policy evidence

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State, Part 2, facility, payer, and program-specific privacy overlays
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Public HIPAA baseline may be narrowed by stricter state rules, 42 CFR Part 2, minor consent rules, payer/program terms, facility procedures, or sensitive-service policies.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant jurisdiction, program, facility, and legal/privacy policy retrieval before release or final incident conclusion; escalate conflicts to legal/privacy officer authority.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant source-of-truth hierarchy across ROI, EHR audit, IAM, patient portal, disclosure log, and incident systems
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Systems may disagree on requester identity, authorization status, access event details, disclosure history, or incident state.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant source hierarchy and evidence owner map; if absent or conflicting, produce low-confidence escalation rather than selecting a winner.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Final breach determination and notification thresholds
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Breach determinations and notification timing depend on legal/privacy officer risk assessment, jurisdictional requirements, and tenant escalation procedures.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The lane may triage and collect evidence only; it must route final determination, notification, sanctions, and regulator-facing communications to authorized owners.
